description_long: This object is a pickaxe-style tool with a long, cylindrical wooden handle and a double-sided, curved metal head. The handle is dark brown with visible wood grain texture, suggesting it is made from a sturdy hardwood. The metal head is silvery-gray, with both ends tapering to sharp points, ideal for breaking hard surfaces. The head is securely mounted at the top of the handle, with a central socket that appears robust and well-fitted. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements on the tool. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and functionality for heavy-duty urban or construction tasks.
description: A double-sided pickaxe with a wooden handle and a curved, pointed metal head, designed for breaking hard surfaces. The tool is robust and utilitarian, with a dark wood shaft and silvery metal ends.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the curved, pointed metal head and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the metal head and the cylindrical handle, with the head's socket visible at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, showing the full span of the metal head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the slim profile of the head and handle visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with visible grain, no grip or ergonomic features.
attribute_blade_type: Double-sided, curved, pointed metal head with a central socket; both ends taper to sharp points.
